economic_notes: Membertou has extensive commercial operations including fisheries, commercial sales, gaming, trade and convention centre, data centre, geomatics, sport and wellness centre, insurance brokers, lobster operations, and a hotel limited partnership. First Nations Finance Authority borrowing of $191.5M suggests major capital infrastructure development. Promissory note receivable increased from $5.5M to $50.9M due to First Nation Fisheries Interest Group subordinated debt Series A of $42.86M.
notes: Large and complex consolidated entity with 14 fully consolidated subsidiaries and 1 GBE (Membertou Properties Limited Partnership) on modified equity basis. Significant long-term debt of $250.5M primarily from First Nations Finance Authority ($191.5M), suggesting major capital development program. Purchased fishing licenses and permits of $121.8M are intangible assets not amortized. Promissory note receivable jumped from $5.5M to $50.9M due to non-monetary transaction related to First Nation Fisheries Interest Group subordinated debt Series A. Net debt position of $188.3M. Capital assets acquired of $28.1M in year. Remuneration schedule not included in extracted text. Expenses_housing set to 0 as no separate housing line in income statement; infrastructure proxied by repairs and maintenance. No schedule of remuneration provided in the text.
